commit.message:
Fix confusing error message for unsupported output format; remove plain-text as advertised format (#11220)

# Description

This PR improves the CLI output format handling by:

1. **Fixing the confusing error message** — previously, passing an
unsupported output format produced a vague error. Now it clearly tells
the user which format was rejected and lists the supported formats
(`json`, `table`).
2. **Removing `plain-text` as an advertised output format** —
`plain-text` was only accepted by `rad recipe-pack show` and produced
output identical to `table`. It is no longer shown in help text or flag
descriptions.
3. **Maintaining backwards compatibility** — users who pass `--output
plain-text` (e.g. in scripts) will silently get table-formatted output
instead of an error.

## Changes

### Output format normalization
- Added `NormalizeFormat()` in `pkg/cli/output/formats.go` that maps
`"plain-text"` → `"table"`
- Added `FormatPlainText` case in `NewFormatter()` that returns
`TableFormatter` (defense in depth)

### Unified validation
- Rewrote `RequireOutput()` in `pkg/cli/clivalidation.go` to normalize
`"plain-text"` → `"table"` before validation
- Deleted `RequireOutputAllowPlainText()` and
`requireOutputFromFormats()` — no longer needed
- Deleted `AddOutputFlagWithPlainText()` from
`pkg/cli/cmd/commonflags/flags.go`

### Command updates
- `rad recipe-pack show`: switched from `AddOutputFlagWithPlainText` +
`RequireOutputAllowPlainText` to standard `AddOutputFlag` +
`RequireOutput`
- `rad version`: switched from manual flag reading to
`cli.RequireOutput()` for consistent validation; registered output flag
via `commonflags.AddOutputFlag(cmd)` in `NewCommand()`

### Tests
- Updated `Test_RequireOutput`: `"plain-text"` now expects `"table"`
(not an error)
- Removed `Test_RequireOutputAllowPlainText` (function deleted)
- Added `pkg/cli/output/formats_test.go` — tests for `NormalizeFormat`
- Added `pkg/cli/output/formatter_test.go` — tests for `NewFormatter`
including `"plain-text"` case
- Added table format test case in
`pkg/cli/cmd/recipepack/show/show_test.go`

## Backwards Compatibility

| User input | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `rad app list -o json` | JSON output | JSON output (unchanged) |
| `rad app list -o table` | Table output | Table output (unchanged) |
| `rad app list -o plain-text` | Error: unsupported format | **Table
output** (backwards compat) |
| `rad app list -o text` | Error: unsupported format | Error:
unsupported format (unchanged) |
| `rad app list --help` | "json, table" | "json, table" (unchanged) |
| `rad recipe-pack show X -o plain-text` | Table output (accepted) |
Table output (still accepted, normalized) |
| `rad recipe-pack show X -o table` | Error: unsupported format |
**Table output** (now accepted) |
| `rad recipe-pack show --help` | "plain-text, json" | **"json, table"**
(consistent) |

**Key point**: `"plain-text"` was never meaningfully different from
`"table"` — it always produced the same output. This change makes the
CLI consistent while ensuring existing scripts continue to work.

## Type of change

- This pull request fixes a bug in Radius and has an approved issue
(issue link required).

commit.message:
docs: add comprehensive MVP test scenarios (#1)

* docs: add comprehensive MVP test scenarios

Test scenarios covering:
- Authentication & Authorization (Keycloak integration)
- Match Management (CRUD operations)
- Event Management with ownership rules
- Shot image capture for heat maps
- Image storage in Cloud Storage
- Data validation
- Integration tests

Key Requirements:
- Team owners can add all event types to their team
- Opponents can only add score events
- Score events (2/3 points) require shot location image
- Images stored in Google Cloud Storage for heat map generation
- 36 total test cases across 6 categories

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update requirements with shot coordinates instead of images

Key Changes:
- Score events require shot coordinates (x,y) instead of image storage
- User selects position on visual court image UI component
- Only coordinates stored in database (not images)
- Coordinates used to generate player/team heat maps
- Updated data structure with ScoreEvent coordinates field
- Added business rules for event ownership and match lifecycle

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add missed shot events with coordinates

Key Changes:
- New event type: Missed Shot (arremessos que não geraram pontos)
- Missed shots require coordinates (x,y) like made shots
- Opponents can register missed shots for opponent team (not just scores)
- Heat maps now show both made and missed shots
- Updated data structure with MissedShotEvent type
- Added 15+ test cases for missed shot functionality
- Expanded coordinate storage and heat map generation tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add free throw events with FIBA rules

Key Changes:
- New event type: Free Throw (lances livres)
- Free throws based on foul type (personal, technical, flagrant):
  - Personal foul (no bonus): 1 free throw
  - Personal foul (team bonus - 4+ fouls): 2 free throws
  - Technical foul: 1 free throw
  - Flagrant foul: 2 free throws + ball possession
- Each free throw: 1 point (made) or 0 (missed)
- NO coordinates (fixed line position)
- Tracked separately: made vs missed free throws
- Opponents can register free throws for opponent team
- Updated data structure with FreeThrowEvent and team foul tracking
- Added 13+ test cases for free throw functionality
- Bonus calculation after 4 team fouls per period (FIBA rules)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add period timing and event timestamp tracking

Key Changes:
- Match structure: 4 periods (10 minutes each - FIBA standard)
- Each period tracked: number, startTime, endTime
- Each event includes 3 timestamps:
  - Absolute timestamp (when event occurred)
  - Period number (1-4)
  - Period timestamp (seconds within period, 0-600s)
- Period timestamps enable:
  - Score evolution graphs over time
  - Points per quarter analysis
  - Timeline visualizations
- Updated data structure with Period type

commit.message:
perf: eliminate per-tick O(N log N) full-state hashing to scale to 50k units (#79)

Per-tick `hashWorldState()` sorted and serialized all N entities every
tick — the dominant cost at scale (~350ms/tick at 50k units, 70-80% of
tick time). Determinism is guaranteed by command ordering alone; full
state can be verified by replay from the initial anchor.

## Chain hash model change

**Before (O(N log N) per tick):**
```js
const stateHash = hashWorldState(newState);  // sort + hash all entities
chainHash = sha256(prevChainHash, frameHash, stateHash);
```

**After (O(1) per tick):**
```js
// Initial anchor still computed once via hashWorldState(initialState)
chainHash = sha256(prevChainHash, frameHash, String(newState.tick));
```

`HASH_VERSION` bumped to `2`. `stateHash` removed from proof records and
`encodeProofCanonical`.

## Changes

- **`executor.js`** — remove per-tick `hashWorldState`; O(1) chain hash;
`HASH_VERSION → 2`; drop `stateHash` from proof and canonical encoding;
update `replay()` to match
- **`verifyLedger.js`** (root + golden bundle) — match new chain hash
algorithm; remove `stateHash` verification step
- **`core/sim_v1.cjs`** — replace `sha256(stableStringify(snap.units))`
with `sha256(envelopeHash + tick)`; only `frameId` + `commands` hashed
per envelope (tamper detection for envelope metadata handled separately
by `prevChainHash` mismatch check)
- **`core/spec/sim_spec_v1.md`** — document new two-phase hash chain
(once-anchored initial state + O(1) per-tick)
- **Regenerated artifacts** — golden bundle (`ledger.json`,
`envelopes.json`, `public.pem`), `golden_hashes_v0.json`, contract/locks
hashes, docs

[DMS-952] Add preflight seed validation with row-level diff reporting to ddl provision (#860)

* Add preflight seed validation with row-level diff reporting to ddl provision

* Add defensive guards and cross-reference docs for preflight seed validation

* Add EffectiveSchema preflight validation and improve seed mismatch diagnostics

* Replace generic hash-mismatch throw with SchemaHashChecker.ValidateOrThrow

In both PgsqlDatabaseProvisioner and MssqlDatabaseProvisioner, the
PreflightSeedValidation hash guard now delegates to SchemaHashChecker
instead of throwing a generic message. This ensures the error includes
both stored and expected hash values for better diagnostics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fail fast when EffectiveSchema singleton row is missing in preflight checks

If the dms.EffectiveSchema table exists but the singleton row (Id=1) is
missing, PreflightSchemaHashCheck and PreflightSeedValidation now throw
InvalidOperationException instead of silently skipping validation. This
prevents stale seed data from a partial provisioning from going undetected.
Integration tests added for both PostgreSQL and MSSQL dialects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Make string_agg/STRING_AGG deterministic with ORDER BY in seed validation diagnostics

Add ORDER BY inside aggregate functions so mismatch diagnostic sample lists
(ResourceKeyIds, ProjectEndpointNames) produce deterministic output in both
PostgreSQL and MSSQL dialects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove working files

* Fail fast when required seed tables are missing in preflight validation

Add table-existence check in PreflightSeedValidation() for both Pgsql and
Mssql provisioners. When dms.EffectiveSchema exists but ResourceKey or
SchemaComponent tables are missing, throws a clear error naming the missing
tables and recommending drop/recreate, instead of a raw provider exception.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Make SeedValidator diff output deterministic with ORDER BY in dictionary iterations

Add .OrderBy() to all dictionary iterations in ValidateResourceKeysOrThrow and
ValidateSchemaComponentsOrThrow so Missing/Unexpected/Modified sections are
emitted in stable sorted order (ResourceKeyId ascending, ProjectEndpointName
ordinal). This stabilizes CI logs and eliminates flakiness from .NET Dictionary
enumeration order non-determinism.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add integration tests for missing seed-table detection in preflight validation

Covers both pgsql and mssql: drops ResourceKey (with CASCADE/FK cleanup)
and SchemaComponent after provisioning, then verifies the re-provisioning
run fails fast with the expected diagnostic message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove AppliedAt from EffectiveSchema INSERT column list in design doc

The column is intentionally omitted from the INSERT; it is populated by
the DEFAULT constraint DF_EffectiveSchema_AppliedAt using the dialect-
appropriate current-timestamp expression. Aligns doc with SeedDmlEmitter
implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Clarify insert-if-missing vs preflight fail-fast in design doc

The emitted insert-if-missing SQL provides idempotency for standalone
script execution. The ddl provision preflight check is an additional
safety layer that fails fast on missing singleton rows (partial/corrupt
state). Updated ddl-generation.md to document both behaviors clearly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Harden FullDdlEmitter segment concatenation with newline boundary guarantees

Replace raw string concatenation with JoinSegments helper that ensures
a newline boundary between each non-empty DDL segment. SqlWriter.Canonicalize
does not guarantee trailing newlines, so without this a future emitter change
could silently fuse the last line of one segment with the first line of the next.

Adds 6 unit tests exercising the JoinSegments contract (trailing newlines,
missing newlines, empty segments, single segment).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Define dialect-aware statement_count semantics in ddl-generator-testing spec

Expand the bare statement_count bullet in ddl.manifest.json spec with
explicit per-dialect definitions: PostgreSQL counts semicolons outside
dollar-quoted blocks; MSSQL counts semicolons before first GO plus
one per non-empty GO-delimited batch. Matches existing DdlManifestEmitter
implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix misleading --ddl-manifest default comment (was "default true", actual is false)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Expand PreflightSeedValidation XML docs to document all failure modes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update DDL manifest golden files with current normalized_sql_sha256 hashes

* Extract shared preflight logic into DatabaseProvisionerBase abstract class

Deduplicate PreflightSchemaHashCheck and PreflightSeedValidation between
Pgsql and Mssql provisioners using DbConnection/DbCommand abstractions.
Dialect-specific SQL strings are captured in a DialectSql record;
concrete classes provide connection creation and non-shared methods.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move EffectiveSchemaHash preflight from Phase 7 to Phase 0 in generated SQL

Standalone .sql scripts (psql -f / sqlcmd -i) now fail fast before any DDL
is applied when the database has a different schema hash. Added
SeedDmlEmitter.EmitPreflightOnly() and FullDdlEmitter.WrapPhase0() to
prepend the check before core DDL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove redundant PreflightSchemaHashCheck call from DdlProvisionCommand

PreflightSeedValidation already validates the schema hash internally,
making the standalone PreflightSchemaHashCheck call an unnecessary
extra database round-trip. The method is retained on the interface
for potential standalone use.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Chunk VALUES clauses in SeedDmlEmitter to avoid SQL Server 1000-row limit

Split EmitResourceKeyExpectedValuesSubquery and EmitSchemaComponentExpectedValuesSubquery
into 999-row chunks joined with UNION ALL when row count exceeds the SQL Server
VALUES table constructor limit. Rows <= 999 produce identical SQL to before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix diagnostic ORDER BY to sort ResourceKeyId numerically

The string_agg/STRING_AGG ORDER BY in ResourceKey diagnostic
subqueries was sorting cast-to-text IDs lexicographically
(e.g. 10 before 2). Added a numeric column to the subquery
so the aggregated comma-separated list is ordered correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Preserve newline and carriage-return in SanitizeForConsole

SanitizeForConsole stripped all control characters including
 and ,
flattening multi-line diff reports from SeedValidator into unreadable
single lines. Exempt
 and  from the control-character filter so
CLI output preserves line breaks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Document all failure modes for PreflightSchemaHashCheck and ValidateOrThrow

Update XML docs on IDatabaseProvisioner.PreflightSchemaHashCheck to list
all four outcomes including the singleton-row-missing corrupt state.
Clarify SchemaHashChecker.ValidateOrThrow null semantics and caller
responsibility for detecting table-exists-but-no-row conditions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* DMS-952 Guard schema-hash preflight on fresh DB

Pgsql: guard EffectiveSchema preflight with to_regclass

Mssql: guard EffectiveSchema preflight with OBJECT_ID

SeedValidator: strip CR/LF from diff values

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix concurrent worker race conditions in BastionBootstrapWorker and ClusterActionWorker (#746)

* Initial plan

* fix: prevent apt lock conflicts in concurrent BastionBootstrapWorker instances

Use SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED inside a transaction to atomically
claim pending bastion servers, ensuring each server is processed by
exactly one worker instance even when archer.instances > 1.

Co-authored-by: allanice001 <700853+allanice001@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent concurrent cluster_action workers from processing the same cluster

Use SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED inside a transaction to atomically
claim a cluster before starting any long-running provisioning work.
Workers that arrive for a cluster already locked (or already in
bootstrapping/provisioning state) fail gracefully with a clear message,
preventing duplicate make-target runs and Docker/apt lock conflicts.

Co-authored-by: allanice001 <700853+allanice001@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: allanice001 <700853+allanice001@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(plugins): harden ABI with version validation and compatibility checks (#227)

Add forward-compatible ABI version negotiation:
- Add abi_version_major/minor/pack helpers and is_abi_compatible() to
  openracing-plugin-abi for packed major.minor version checks
- Add PluginHeader::is_compatible() and version_mismatch_message() for
  clear error reporting on ABI mismatches
- Add check_abi_compatibility_packed() to native plugin crate for
  forward-compatible loading (same major, older minor)
- Add AbiCheckResult::ForwardCompatible variant with is_loadable() helper
- Add PluginError::AbiMismatch variant for clear error propagation

Add comprehensive plugin ABI hardening tests (60 tests):
- ABI version validation: packed version roundtrip, forward-compat,
  major/minor mismatch detection, clear error messages
- Capability enforcement: read/modify/DSP/filesystem/network scoping,
  inter-plugin comm, budget limits, class restrictions
- Loading edge cases: corrupted/empty/truncated WASM, wrong headers,
  missing exports, init failures, crash recovery, fuel exhaustion,
  max instance limits, concurrent loading
- Communication: host→plugin input, plugin→host results, dt parameter,
  memory roundtrip, telemetry serialization, stats tracking,
  hot-reload state preservation, large iteration stability

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add deep tests for hardware watchdog and safety interlock system (#226)

Add 117 tests across two new test files:

watchdog_safety_deep_tests.rs (54 tests):
- Watchdog feed timing at boundary conditions (just before/after timeout)
- Rapid feed/starve cycles and near-timeout rescue patterns
- Full recovery after fault (timeout -> safe-state -> reset -> re-arm)
- Concurrent watchdog access (atomic state reads, concurrent feeds, arm races)
- Different timeout configurations (min/max/builder/invalid)
- Exhaustive state machine transitions (all valid paths)
- Invalid transition rejection (all forbidden state changes)
- State persistence across reset scenarios
- Diagnostic output validation for each state

watchdog_interlock_deep_tests.rs (63 tests):
- Communication loss detection (USB stall via consecutive failures and timeout)
- Watchdog starvation -> safe state via soft-stop torque ramp
- Corrupted command rejection (NaN, infinity, plugin overrun, timing violation)
- Multiple simultaneous fault handling and overwrite behavior
- Fault recovery sequences (context lifecycle, timeout, retry, cancel)
- Safety system with FMEA matrix validation and fault statistics
- Torque limiting during fault conditions (monotonic decrease, force stop)
- Safe state always results in zero torque output
- Threshold validation and custom threshold application
- Fault classification properties (severity, recoverability, action consistency)

All tests return Result (no unwrap/expect), pass clippy with -D warnings,
and are formatted with rustfmt.

commit.message:
fix: wire RawMode::Field through lib_api decode path (#381)

Previously, RawMode::Field was accepted by the CLI and produced
record-level __raw_b64, but did not emit field-level raw entries
({FIELD_NAME}_raw_b64) through the lib_api decode path.

Add field-level raw capture to both process_scalar_field_standard
and process_scalar_field_with_scratch: when RawMode::Field is active,
each decoded scalar field gets a sibling {name}_raw_b64 entry with
base64-encoded raw bytes.

Add 3 new integration tests proving field-level raw capture works.

Closes #372

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: wire bwz_encode through lib_api encode path (#380)

Previously, the bwz_encode flag on EncodeOptions was only respected
in the json.rs streaming encoder. The lib_api encode_zoned_decimal_field
function always used encode_zoned_decimal_with_format_and_policy which
ignores BLANK WHEN ZERO semantics.

When field.blank_when_zero is true and options.bwz_encode is enabled,
now delegates to encode_zoned_decimal_with_bwz which produces
all-space output for zero values.

Add 3 new integration tests:
- encode_bwz_zero_value_produces_spaces
- encode_bwz_nonzero_value_normal
- encode_bwz_disabled_zero_value_normal

Closes #371
